parsed_review.summary: CP00-493 is a descriptor-only RP16 governance foundation pack (150 units, RP16.P04.M06.S05-RP16.P05.M03.S06, risk C) and does NOT claim production readiness: manifest sets production_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, runtime_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review. The descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data claims are well supported. New factories createGovernanceCoreCp493P04CloseoutP05Foundation{CaseSet,Descriptor} return deeply frozen descriptor objects built on the CP492 upstream with explicit attestations (writes_product_state=false, dispatches_*_runtime=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). Validators validateGovernanceCoreCp493Coverage and ...P04CloseoutP05FoundationDescriptor enforce exact 150-unit coverage, all deliverable/phase/micro distributions, per-row descriptor_only=true/runtime_execution=false, and the authority boundary; model.test.js adds three meaningful CP493 tests; validate-rp16-governance-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the committed contract JSON to the code-generated descriptor (drift guard) and asserts historical bindings end at CP00-493. Authority boundary holds throughout: claude_is_final_approval=false, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, claude packet read_only=true with allowed_tools [Read,Grep,Glob], human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim=true. Hermes is used only as a deterministic evidence packet (gate H16, receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write) — not product runtime. CP00-493 genuinely exists in the live closeout-pack-plan.json (line 408; deviation_from_plan=false), so coverage validation is not circular. No secrets/PII in sources (only no_real_data=true flags). All expected validation commands are recorded with exit_code 0 in command-evidence.json, and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid, non-empty JSON. The three post-review evidence artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is correct per the documented hardening_sequence (they are produced after this read-only review). Claude remains the independent read-only reviewer and not the final approver. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one P3 informational note.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts referenced by manifest are not present yet (expected by the hardening sequence)
findings.details: manifest.json required_pack_artifacts/evidence_refs reference docs/closeout-packs/cp00-493/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, none of which exist on disk yet (Glob of docs/closeout-packs/cp00-493/ returns only manifest.json and command-evidence.json). This is correct and expected, not a missing deliverable: command-evidence.json notes flag these as post-review gated, and manifest pack_level_claude_review.hardening_sequence orders read_only_claude_run_raw_capture -> normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit, i.e., these artifacts are produced after this read-only review run. At review time their absence is the designed state.
findings.recommendation: No action required from this review. The final closeout gate (npm run closeout-pack:validate, run after the accepted review per command-evidence notes) must confirm all three artifacts exist and validate before commit.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected / non-blocking. Verified this matches the documented hardening sequence; flagged only for transparency so the not-yet-present evidence files are not mistaken for a missing deliverable.
